# Wildlife Discovered

> Small-group wildlife photography tours across Canada, led by professional guide Erik Bertelsen.

## Pages
- [Home](/): Overview of Wildlife Discovered and our wildlife photography tours across Canada.
- [Loon Photography Tours](/tours/loon-photography-tours): Photograph common loons and chicks from a purpose-built boat in Northern Ontario.
- [Algonquin Park Wildlife Photography Tours](/tours/algonquin-park-photography-tours): Photograph moose, bald eagles, and loons by boat in Algonquin Park.
- [Polar Bear Photography Tours](/tours/polar-bear-photography-tour): Ground-level polar bear photography in Churchill, Manitoba.
- [Moose Photography Tours](/tours/moose-photography-tours): Photograph moose feeding on lily pads from a boat in Algonquin Park, Ontario.
- [Waterfowl & Swan Photography Workshops](/tours/waterfowl-and-swan-photography-tours): Low-angle photography of swans, diving ducks, osprey, and terns.
- [Contact](/contact): Get in touch to plan your wildlife photography adventure.

## About
Wildlife Discovered offers small group wildlife photography tours across Canada, led by professional guide and photographer Erik Bertelsen. Tours focus on authentic, wild encounters — no zoos, enclosures, or staged settings.

## Tours Offered

### Loon Photography Tours (July)
- Location: Northern Ontario, 2.5 hours north of Toronto
- Duration: 2–6 days
- Group size: Maximum 4 photographers
- Photograph common loons and chicks from a purpose-built boat at water level

### Algonquin Park Wildlife Photography Tours (June)
- Location: Algonquin Provincial Park, Ontario
- Duration: 2–6 days
- Group size: Maximum 4 photographers
- Photograph moose, bald eagles, loons, and boreal wildlife by boat

### Polar Bear Photography Tours (October–November)
- Location: Churchill, Manitoba
- Duration: 6–8 days
- Group size: 5 photographers
- Ground-level polar bear photography with arctic fox, ptarmigan, and northern lights

### Moose Photography Tours (June)
- Location: Algonquin Provincial Park, Ontario
- Duration: 2–6 days
- Group size: Maximum 4 photographers
- Photograph moose feeding on aquatic vegetation at water level from a purpose-built boat

### Waterfowl & Swan Photography Workshops (March–April)
- Location: Southern Ontario
- Duration: 2–3 days
- Group size: Maximum 4 photographers
- Photograph diving ducks, fighting swans, osprey, and terns from a low-angle boat

### Swan Photography Tour (March)
- Location: Southern Ontario
- Duration: 1–2 days
- Group size: Maximum 4 photographers
- Photograph tundra swans during spring migration

## Contact
- Email: wildlifediscovered@outlook.com
- Website: https://wildlifediscovered.com
- Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/wildlife.discovered
